tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57976,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ResponseContext API, ItemExtensions, and resolveReferences in README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #57974 significantly refactored the input-access API: - `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` return type changed from `IReadOnlyList` to `IReadOnlyList` and gained a new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter - `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync()` was added as a new convenience method - `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` was added as a new **public** extension method - `CreateResponse.GetInputText()` was made **internal** (was previously accessible; callers must migrate to `context.GetInputTextAsync()`) The CHANGELOG and snippet files were updated, but the README Key Concepts section was not updated to document these new/changed APIs. ## Gaps Found - **No `ResponseContext` key concept section** — `ResponseContext` is one of the three parameters to `CreateAsync()` and is now the recommended entrypoint for all input access, but it has no dedicated subsection in the README. Users reading the README see it used in examples without understanding what it provides. - **`resolveReferences` parameter is undocumented** — The new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter for `GetInputItemsAsync()` is not explained anywhere in the README. Users need to know what passing `false` means (item references returned as `ItemReferenceParam` without resolution) and when to use it. - **`ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` is not mentioned** — This new public static extension method is listed in the CHANGELOG but not in the README Key Concepts. Users who already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call) should know they can call `.GetInputText()` directly without going through `ResponseContext`. - **`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` is missing from Key Concepts** — Also CHANGELOG-listed but absent from the README. Advanced users who need to access the raw (unresolved) expanded input as `OutputItem` types need to know this exists and how it differs from `context.GetInputItemsAsync()`. - **`GetHistoryAsync()` is not mentioned in the README** — It is shown in `Sample5_ConversationHistory.md` but the README Key Concepts section has no mention of history access, even though it's a key multi-turn capability. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit (add a new subsection and extend the existing Key Concepts) Add a new `### ResponseContext` subsection **between** the existing `### ResponseHandler` subsection and `### ResponseEventStream`. Insert: ```markdown ### ResponseContext `ResponseContext` is the second parameter to `CreateAsync`. It provides the handler with the response identifier, the resolved input items, conversation history, isolation keys, and forwarded client metadata. **Accessing input text (recommended for most handlers):** ``` // Resolves input items (including item references) and returns all text as a single string. var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] individual input items:** ``` // Returns IReadOnlyList. By default, item references are resolved to their Item subtypes. var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Pass resolveReferences: false to skip reference resolution and preserve ItemReferenceParam objects. var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] text from an already-resolved item list (`ItemExtensions`):** If you already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call), you can extract text without a second async call: ``` // Extension method on IEnumerable — no async needed. string text = items.GetInputText(); ``` **Accessing conversation history (multi-turn):** ``` // Returns IReadOnlyList from previous turns (via previous_response_id or conversation context). var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] — raw input access (`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`):** When you need the raw, unresolved input as `OutputItem` types (before provider resolution), use the extension directly on the request: ``` // Returns a List from the raw request body. Item references remain as ItemReferenceParam. var rawInput = request.GetInputExpanded(); ``` Use `context.GetInputItemsAsync()` in preference to `GetInputExpanded()` for normal handlers — it handles reference resolution and caching automatically. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets For each snippet placeholder added in Step 1, write the corresponding snippet in a test file. **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` — Edit (add new test methods) Add after the existing `Implement_EchoHandler` test method block: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetInputText() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputText var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(inputText, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetInputItems() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputItems var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(items, Is.Not.Null); Assert.That(rawItems, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public void ItemExtensions_GetInputText() { var items = new List(); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensionsGetInputText string text = items.GetInputText(); #endregion Assert.That(text, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetHistory() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetHistory var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(history, Is.Not.Null); } ``` For `Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputExpanded`, add a thin test using `CreateResponseExtensions.GetInputExpanded()` with a constructed `CreateResponse` instance (the snippet only requires it to compile). ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections. The only changes needed are: - Add the `### ResponseContext` subsection under **Key concepts** (between `### ResponseHandler` and `### ResponseEventStream`) - Add `ItemExtensions` and `GetInputExpanded()` to the new subsection as shown above ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm the README `Snippet:` placeholders are replaced with the extracted code 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm no drift 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24172924334/agentic_workflow) · ● 405.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57976","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T04:54:23Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T04:54:23Z"}

{"number":57976,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ResponseContext API, ItemExtensions, and resolveReferences in README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #57974 significantly refactored the input-access API: - `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` return type changed from `IReadOnlyList` to `IReadOnlyList` and gained a new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter - `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync()` was added as a new convenience method - `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` was added as a new **public** extension method - `CreateResponse.GetInputText()` was made **internal** (was previously accessible; callers must migrate to `context.GetInputTextAsync()`) The CHANGELOG and snippet files were updated, but the README Key Concepts section was not updated to document these new/changed APIs. ## Gaps Found - **No `ResponseContext` key concept section** — `ResponseContext` is one of the three parameters to `CreateAsync()` and is now the recommended entrypoint for all input access, but it has no dedicated subsection in the README. Users reading the README see it used in examples without understanding what it provides. - **`resolveReferences` parameter is undocumented** — The new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter for `GetInputItemsAsync()` is not explained anywhere in the README. Users need to know what passing `false` means (item references returned as `ItemReferenceParam` without resolution) and when to use it. - **`ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` is not mentioned** — This new public static extension method is listed in the CHANGELOG but not in the README Key Concepts. Users who already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call) should know they can call `.GetInputText()` directly without going through `ResponseContext`. - **`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` is missing from Key Concepts** — Also CHANGELOG-listed but absent from the README. Advanced users who need to access the raw (unresolved) expanded input as `OutputItem` types need to know this exists and how it differs from `context.GetInputItemsAsync()`. - **`GetHistoryAsync()` is not mentioned in the README** — It is shown in `Sample5_ConversationHistory.md` but the README Key Concepts section has no mention of history access, even though it's a key multi-turn capability. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit (add a new subsection and extend the existing Key Concepts) Add a new `### ResponseContext` subsection **between** the existing `### ResponseHandler` subsection and `### ResponseEventStream`. Insert: ```markdown ### ResponseContext `ResponseContext` is the second parameter to `CreateAsync`. It provides the handler with the response identifier, the resolved input items, conversation history, isolation keys, and forwarded client metadata. **Accessing input text (recommended for most handlers):** ``` // Resolves input items (including item references) and returns all text as a single string. var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] individual input items:** ``` // Returns IReadOnlyList. By default, item references are resolved to their Item subtypes. var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Pass resolveReferences: false to skip reference resolution and preserve ItemReferenceParam objects. var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] text from an already-resolved item list (`ItemExtensions`):** If you already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call), you can extract text without a second async call: ``` // Extension method on IEnumerable — no async needed. string text = items.GetInputText(); ``` **Accessing conversation history (multi-turn):** ``` // Returns IReadOnlyList from previous turns (via previous_response_id or conversation context). var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] — raw input access (`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`):** When you need the raw, unresolved input as `OutputItem` types (before provider resolution), use the extension directly on the request: ``` // Returns a List from the raw request body. Item references remain as ItemReferenceParam. var rawInput = request.GetInputExpanded(); ``` Use `context.GetInputItemsAsync()` in preference to `GetInputExpanded()` for normal handlers — it handles reference resolution and caching automatically. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets For each snippet placeholder added in Step 1, write the corresponding snippet in a test file. **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` — Edit (add new test methods) Add after the existing `Implement_EchoHandler` test method block: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetInputText() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputText var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(inputText, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetInputItems() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputItems var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(items, Is.Not.Null); Assert.That(rawItems, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public void ItemExtensions_GetInputText() { var items = new List(); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensionsGetInputText string text = items.GetInputText(); #endregion Assert.That(text, Is.Not.Null); } [Test] public async Task ResponseContext_GetHistory() { var context = new ResponseContext("test-id"); #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetHistory var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] #endregion Assert.That(history, Is.Not.Null); } ``` For `Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputExpanded`, add a thin test using `CreateResponseExtensions.GetInputExpanded()` with a constructed `CreateResponse` instance (the snippet only requires it to compile). ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections. The only changes needed are: - Add the `### ResponseContext` subsection under **Key concepts** (between `### ResponseHandler` and `### ResponseEventStream`) - Add `ItemExtensions` and `GetInputExpanded()` to the new subsection as shown above ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm the README `Snippet:` placeholders are replaced with the extracted code 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm no drift 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24172924334/agentic_workflow) · ● 405.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57976","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T04:54:23Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T04:54:23Z"}

{"total_count":23,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"brandonh-msft","id":20270743,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/brandonh-msft","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1069434707,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention","name":"Service Streaming update result in "The input does not contain any JSON tokens" exception","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

Sometimes (not always) when I iterate over `AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate>` I get an error stating:

```
The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true. LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
```

* I have the same problem with both `gpt-4.1` and `gpt-5`
* **Worth pointing out that it works if I use the same agent and prompt from the AI Foundry Agent  playground**

The error comes after `UpdateKind` = RunInProgress

```csharp
PersistentThreadMessage messageResponse = _client.Messages.CreateMessage(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    role: MessageRole.User,
    content: request.Prompt);

AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> stream = _client.Runs.CreateRunStreamingAsync(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    agentId: _agent.Id
);

await foreach (StreamingUpdate streamingUpdate in stream) // This is where the error occurs after RunInProgress
{
      _logger.LogInformation(streamingUpdate.UpdateKind.ToString());
}
```

### Expected behavior

If this is due to a problem with the payload, such as an empty response from the LLM or a throttling error, I would expect to see this error

### Actual behavior

I assume the response from the LLM is not in an expected format and that this is an internal bug. 

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Environment

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync throws JSON parsing error with gpt-5 but works with gpt-4o","body":"### Library name and version

Microsoft.Agents.AI 1.0.0-preview.260128.1

### Describe the bug

We’re encountering a JSON parsing exception when using ChatClientAgent.RunAsync with the gpt-5 model. The same code works correctly when switching to gpt-4o.

This appears to be either a service-side incompatibility with Agents or an SDK parsing issue when handling the final response block.

### Expected behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync returns an AgentResponse

### Actual behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync throws an exception:

`The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true.
LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
`

### Reproduction Steps

```    
    var chatMessage = new ChatMessage
    {
      Role = ChatRole.User,
      Contents = [new DataContent(image, "image/png")]
    };

    var response = await _chatClientAgent.RunAsync(chatMessage, session, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Environment

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README — specifically missing coverage of `ResponseContext` APIs, the `resolveReferences` parameter, `ItemExtensions.GetInputText`, `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`, and `GetHistoryAsync`, all introduced or changed in PR #57974.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/)
**Affected API:** `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync`, `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync`, `ItemExtensions.GetInputText`, `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`, `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync`
**Scenarios:**
- Users reading the README don't understand what `ResponseContext` provides or how to use it
- The new `resolveReferences` parameter on `GetInputItemsAsync` is undocumented, leaving users unaware of the distinction between resolved and raw item access
- `ItemExtensions.GetInputText` is CHANGELOG-listed but not in README Key Concepts, so users may make redundant async calls
- `GetHistoryAsync` is shown in samples but absent from Key Concepts, making multi-turn support hard to discover

**Root ask:** Add a `### ResponseContext` subsection to the README Key Concepts section documenting all new/changed input-access APIs introduced in PR #57974.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue triggered by commit a9a95cda (PR #57974). No runtime bug is present.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57974 to confirm the full API surface of the changes (return types, new parameters, visibility changes)
2. Add the `### ResponseContext` subsection to README.md between `### ResponseHandler` and `### ResponseEventStream` as described in the implementation guide in the issue body
3. Add corresponding snippet tests in `tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to validate placeholder replacement
4. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue is about an Azure SDK client library (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`), not a management or provisioning library
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The package lives in `sdk/agentserver/`, which is mapped to `%AI Agents` in CODEOWNERS; confirmed by three quality reference issues (e.g., #57183, #55585) carrying `Client` + `AI Agents`
- **Confidence:** High — Package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue body; no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — only service label `AI Agents` needs to match; this entry covers all AI Agents issues
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment (issue not customer-reported, so "needs-team-attention" was not added)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from line 1228 upward; all management-specific entries (lines ~912–1228) were skipped as they require `%Mgmt`; first matching entry encountered was `%AI Agents` at line 85
</details>
